page.content: In the study of DAMP, various disease models illustrate the effects of deletion. For Biglycan, renal inflammation (unilateral ureteral obstruction) showed reduced kidney damage, as referenced in [87]. In the sepsis model using LPS or zymosan, deletion provided protection from lethality, referenced in [23, 87]. FNEDA demonstrated reduced lesion size, number, and macrophage infiltration in atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet), as noted in [139]. MRP8/MRP14 indicated reduced bacterial dissemination, systemic inflammation, and liver damage during abdominal sepsis (E. coli), with reference [140]. Furthermore, arterial injury (femoral wire insertion) revealed reduced inflammatory cell infiltration and neointimal formation, referenced in [141]. Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet) was also associated with attenuated atherosclerotic lesions and macrophage accumulation in plaques, as mentioned in [141].

ureteral obstruction) showed reduced kidney damage, as referenced in [87]. In the sepsis model using LPS or zymosan, deletion
provided protection from lethality, referenced in [23, 87]. FNEDA demonstrated reduced lesion size, number, and macrophage
infiltration in atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet), as noted in [139]. MRP8/MRP14 indicated reduced bacterial
dissemination, systemic inflammation, and liver damage during abdominal sepsis (E. coli), with reference [140]. Furthermore,
arterial injury (femoral wire insertion) revealed reduced inflammatory cell infiltration and neointimal formation, referenced in
[141]. Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet) was also associated with attenuated atherosclerotic lesions and
macrophage accumulation in plaques, as mentioned in [141].

cells.cell_text: Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet)
Reduced lesion size, number and macrophage infiltration
Reduced bacterial dissemination, systemic inflammation, liver damage
Reduced inflammatory cell infiltration and neotintimal formation
Atherosclerosis (Apo E deficient, high fat diet)
Attenuated atherosclerotic lesions and macrophage accumulation in plaques
Reduced lesion volume, brain swelling and inflammatory cell infiltration
Reduced neutrophil accumulation and lesion severity
Protected from joint erosion and tissue destruction

page.content: In cerebral ischemia (cerebral artery occlusion), there was reduced lesion volume, brain swelling, and inflammatory cell infiltration, noted in [142]. Lethal sepsis (LPS) provided evidence that deletion protects from lethality, referenced in [143]. Vasculitis (cytokine induced) showed reduced neutrophil accumulation and lesion severity, as per [141]. Tenascin-C in destructive joint inflammation (antigen induced arthritis) was found to protect from joint erosion and tissue destruction, referenced in [24]. Finally, tumorigenesis, when crossed with MMTV/PyV mice, induced smaller tumor nests, as noted in [144].
